join ccal_repl_owner.AR_DIM BKR_AR_DIM ON T.AR_ID = BKR_AR_DIM.AR_ID
and TD >= BKR_AR_DIM.EFF_DT (+) 
and TD < BKR_AR_DIM.END_DT (+))

If BKR_AR_DIM.ORIG_SRC_STM_CD = 'BKRBO1' then populate with T.AR_ID
Else if AP.BKR_AR_ID is not NULL then use ap.BKR_AR_ID
Else join ccsi_owner.AR_GRP_SUBDIM asd ON asd.AR_ID = t.AR_ID
and t.td >= asd.EFF_DT
and t.td < asd.END_DT
and populate with asd.LINKED_BKR_AR_ID
drd_notes: Brokerage arrangement associated with the Arrangement id. If the transaction is for a brokerage arrangement than both will have same value.

JOIN ccsi_owner.ar_grp_subdim ar_grp_subdim ON ar_grp_subdim.ar_id = t.ar_id
AND  t.td >= ar_grp_subdim.eff_dt
AND  t.td < ar_grp_subdim.end_dt
join ccsi_owner.ar_dim bkr_ar_dim on ar_grp_subdim.linked_bkr_ar_id = bkr_ar_dim.ar_id
AND  t.td >= ar_dim.eff_dt
AND  t.td < ar_dim.end_dt
JOIN ssds_dal_owner.fa_number_v fa_number_v1 ON UPPER(TRIM((bkr_ar_dim.own_bsn_ent_cd) = fa_number_v1.fa_number
AND  t.td >= fa_number_v1.effective_date
AND  t.td < fa_number_v1.end_date;
JOIN SSDS_DAL_OWNER.PERSON_AMS_DISCRETIONARY_STATUS_V brk_own_resp_ams_disc on brk_own_resp_ams_disc.EMPLOYEE_ID = fa_number_v1.responsible_party_employee_id
AND t.td >= brk_own_resp_ams_disc.effective_date
AND t.td < brk_own_resp_ams_disc.end_date
drd_notes: The current status indicating whether the Financial Advisor who owns the account has discretionary trading authority. This status reflects the advisor’s approval state and lifecycle stage for discretionary trading privileges, as defined by internal compliance and operational rules. It is used to monitor and enforce regulatory and firm requirements for discretionary account management.

drd_rule:
Use APACSH logic from 'ETL Notes' tab.

For a Transaction, If 2 APACSH records exists
     If (one record is 'APACSH04' & other is 'APACSH11') 
          then skip 'APACSH04' record and load 'APACSH11' but use the values to
          populate sales charge attributes from 'APACSH04' for 5 attributes (Sales Charge Rate Multiplier Number, Sales Charge Rate, Sales Charge Rate Type Code, Sales Charge Rate Type Id, Sales Charge Rate Type Name)
     else exception entire transaction

ccal_repl_owner.txn t
join ccal_repl_owner.apa ap ON ap.exec_id = t.txn_id
left join ccal_repl_owner.cl_val cv ON AP.APA_TP_ID = cv.CL_VAL_ID AND
CASE    WHEN AP.SALE_CHRG_RATE_TP_ID = 2507 
            THEN AP.SALE_CHRG_RATE
drd_notes: Sales charge expressed as a multiplier for the portion represented by the asset package. This column will have values if Sales Charge Rate is expressed as a multipler.

drd_notes:
A step in execution will come into force when an external firm/entitity execute a Step Out trade and allocation is shared between firms. Step-in/Step-out are 2 sides of the same coin. For every step-in there a cooresponding step-out somewhere else. (For retail trading, we (Raymond James) really only do the step-in side of equation. We can do step-outs, but those are pretty rare. Example- a trade of 10000 unit was executed and split between 2 firm- 7500 ABC Corp and 2500 RJ. So RJ would enter a Step In trade of 2500 from ABC Corp.
Expected valus- blank, 0 , I and O

mapping_source_tables:
TRD_CNCLD_F | TRD_CNCLD_F | If there is a record in CCAL_REPL_OWNER.TXN_RLTNP table with TXN.TXN_ID = TXN_RLTNP.TRGT_TXN_ID
and TXN_RLTNP_TP_ID = 69 (Cancel)
and TXN_RLTNP.TRGT_TXN_ID <> TXN_RLTNP.SRC_TXN_ID
then set to 'Y' for both TRGT_TXN_ID and SRC_TXN_ID.

The check should be performed each time when a new record is written into CCAL_REPL_OWNER.TXN_RLTNP with TXN_RLTNP_TP_ID = 69 and update the flag for both TRGT_TXN_ID and SRC_TXN_ID, if TXN_RLTNP.TRGT_TXN_ID <> TXN_RLTNP.SRC_TXN_ID.

Note: TXN_RLTNP.TRGT_TXN_ID <> TXN_RLTNP.SRC_TXN_ID is needed because it is possible that cancellation trade points to itself when the original trade that is being cancelled is not found. In this case Cancelled Flag should not be set to 'Y'
